tax: Placed at genus rank, Siltwalker is defined by composition and formation mechanics, not by colour, size, or danger: a dry static-bound silt column. A new species belongs here only if it shows a dry upright silt body.
ap: The grouping is recognised by a leaning spindle of silt. A field surveyor confirms membership from at least one persistent marker — a self-swept floor and grey film — which survives even when the body disperses.
eco: Siltwalker occupies dry dust shafts. Persistence is elemental rather than reproductive: members form where a dry bed takes a static charge and recur with the bed recharges as dust resettles, sustaining themselves on static earth-charge.
beh: Across the grouping, behaviour is the disturbance-response of the medium: drifts into breathing-space to abrade and choke. Roles, moods, and summoning circumstances are recorded on the entries below, not treated as new taxa.
traitsText: Shared characters: abrasive dust body and air-pressure sense. These are grouping-level traits only; specific resistances, abilities, and field signs belong to each species and species beneath this node.
abilities: Grouping-level abilities are the broad capacities implied by the shared body: abrasion and air-clouding. Named, bounded powers are assigned to the species that actually possess them.
bg: In the Throat, Siltwalker matters because makes still dust shafts a suffocation hazard. It lets survey reports sort abrasion hazards from smother ones without collapsing distinct hazards into one vague label.
g: Siltwalker is the genus-rank grouping of dry abrasive-silt elementals within the True Elementals. Its members share an upright dust column held by static charge that abrades and chokes, and divide below into species groups by sediment fineness and shaft airflow. The dry-country sibling of the Claymaw.
